Grid.description: Arranges child elements in rows and columns
Grid.usage: Best for complex layouts with multiple rows and columns

StackPanel.description: Arranges child elements in a single line horizontally or vertically
StackPanel.usage: Ideal for simple vertical or horizontal layouts

DockPanel.description: Arranges child elements around the edges of the panel
DockPanel.usage: Perfect for application layouts with headers, footers, and main content

Canvas.description: Positions child elements using explicit coordinates

WrapPanel.description: Arranges child elements in sequential position, breaking content to the next line when no more room
WrapPanel.usage: Good for tag-like layouts or flowing content

Panel.description: Base class for all layout panels in AvaloniaUI; can be used directly as a simple layered canvas where children stack on top of each other
Panel.xaml_example: <Panel><Rectangle Fill="LightBlue" Width="200" Height="200"/><TextBlock Text="Overlay text" HorizontalAlignment="Center" VerticalAlignment="Center"/></Panel>
Panel.usage: Layered content or as a base for custom layout panels

UniformGrid.description: Arranges child elements in a grid where all cells have equal size
UniformGrid.xaml_example: <UniformGrid Rows="2" Columns="3"><Button Content="1"/><Button Content="2"/><Button Content="3"/><Button Content="4"/><Button Content="5"/><Button Content="6"/></UniformGrid>
UniformGrid.usage: Equal-sized grids such as button pads, color swatches, or tile layouts

RelativePanel.description: Defines an area within which you can position and align child objects relative to each other or the panel itself
RelativePanel.usage: Responsive form layouts where controls are positioned relative to siblings

GridSplitter.description: Represents a control that redistributes space between rows or columns of a Grid when a user drags it
GridSplitter.xaml_example: <Grid><Grid.ColumnDefinitions><ColumnDefinition Width="*"/><ColumnDefinition Width="Auto"/><ColumnDefinition Width="*"/></Grid.ColumnDefinitions><TextBlock Grid.Column="0" Text="Left"/><GridSplitter Grid.Column="1" Width="5" ResizeDirection="Columns"/><TextBlock Grid.Column="2" Text="Right"/></Grid>
GridSplitter.usage: Resizable split panels and adjustable layout areas

Button.usage: Primary action trigger in user interfaces

TextBox.description: Allows users to enter and edit text
TextBox.usage: Text input for forms and data entry

CheckBox.description: Provides a way to select or deselect an option

RadioButton.description: Allows users to select one option from a group

Slider.description: Allows users to select a numeric value from a range
Slider.usage: Numeric value selection within a range

RepeatButton.description: Represents a button that raises a Click event repeatedly from the time it is pressed until it is released
RepeatButton.usage: Continuous actions such as scrolling, zooming, or incrementing values while held down

ToggleButton.description: Represents a button that can be toggled between checked and unchecked states
ToggleButton.usage: On/off toggle controls such as bold/italic formatting or feature toggles

SplitButton.description: Represents a button with a primary action and a secondary dropdown section for additional actions
SplitButton.xaml_example: <SplitButton Content="Save" Command="{Binding SaveCommand}"><SplitButton.Flyout><MenuFlyout><MenuItem Header="Save As..." Command="{Binding SaveAsCommand}"/><MenuItem Header="Export" Command="{Binding ExportCommand}"/></MenuFlyout></SplitButton.Flyout></SplitButton>
SplitButton.usage: Primary actions with additional related options accessible from a dropdown

ToggleSplitButton.description: A variant of SplitButton that can be toggled between checked and unchecked states while also having a dropdown flyout
ToggleSplitButton.usage: Toggle formatting options that also expose related choices via a flyout

MaskedTextBox.description: Represents a text box that restricts user input to a specific pattern defined by a mask
MaskedTextBox.usage: Structured input such as phone numbers, dates, postal codes, or credit card numbers

TextBlock.usage: Displaying labels, titles, and read-only text

ProgressBar.description: Shows the progress of an operation

SelectableTextBlock.description: Displays read-only text that can be selected and copied by the user
SelectableTextBlock.usage: Displaying text that users may need to copy, such as error messages, IDs, or URLs

PathIcon.description: Displays an icon defined by a vector path geometry in Avalonia's StreamGeometry / mini-language format; scales cleanly at any size without pixelation
PathIcon.usage: Scalable vector icons; the Data property accepts Avalonia StreamGeometry mini-language strings (same as WPF Path.Data). For complex icons, use PathGeometry markup or convert SVG paths via an SVG-to-path tool.

ComboBox.description: Provides a dropdown list of items for selection
ComboBox.usage: Single selection from a list of options

ListBox.description: Displays a list of items that can be selected
ListBox.usage: Single or multiple selection from a visible list

DataGrid.description: Displays data in a tabular format with sorting and editing capabilities

ItemsControl.description: Displays a collection of items using a configurable item template; base class for all item-based controls
ItemsControl.xaml_example: <ItemsControl ItemsSource="{Binding Items}"><ItemsControl.ItemTemplate><DataTemplate><Border BorderBrush="Gray" BorderThickness="1" Margin="2" Padding="4"><TextBlock Text="{Binding Name}"/></Border></DataTemplate></ItemsControl.ItemTemplate></ItemsControl>
ItemsControl.usage: Displaying collections of data without built-in selection; fully customizable via templates

ItemsRepeater.description: A virtualizing items layout control that repeats a data template for each item in a collection with no built-in UI chrome
ItemsRepeater.usage: High-performance virtualized lists and grids; use when ItemsControl does not provide enough customization

Border.description: Draws a border, background, or both around a child element
Border.xaml_example: <Border BorderBrush="Black" BorderThickness="2" CornerRadius="5" Background="LightGray"><TextBlock Text="Bordered content"/></Border>
Border.usage: Visual boundaries and backgrounds for other controls

Popup.description: Displays content on top of existing content, within the bounds of the application window
Popup.xaml_example: <Popup IsOpen="{Binding ShowPopup}" PlacementTarget="{Binding ElementName=MyButton}"><Border Background="White" BorderBrush="Black" BorderThickness="1"><TextBlock Text="Popup content"/></Border></Popup>
Popup.usage: Tooltips, dropdown menus, and overlay content

ButtonSpinner.description: Represents a spinner control that wraps arbitrary content and provides up/down spin buttons
ButtonSpinner.usage: Wrapping any content with up/down navigation buttons; foundation for NumericUpDown

TreeView.description: Displays hierarchical data in a tree structure with expanding/collapsing nodes
TreeView.usage: File explorers, organizational hierarchies, nested data structures

Menu.description: Represents a Windows menu control that enables hierarchical organization of menu items
Menu.xaml_example: <Menu><MenuItem Header="File"><MenuItem Header="New" Command="{Binding NewCommand}"/><MenuItem Header="Open" Command="{Binding OpenCommand}"/></MenuItem></Menu>
Menu.usage: Application main menus and menu bars

ContextMenu.description: Represents a pop-up menu that enables a control to expose functionality specific to the context of the control

MenuItem.description: Represents a selectable item inside a Menu or ContextMenu

Calendar.description: Represents a control that enables a user to select a date by using a visual calendar display
Calendar.xaml_example: <Calendar SelectedDate="{Binding SelectedDate}" DisplayDateStart="{Binding MinDate}" DisplayDateEnd="{Binding MaxDate}"/>
Calendar.usage: Date selection with visual calendar interface

DatePicker.description: Represents a control that allows the user to select a date by using a spinner control
DatePicker.usage: Compact date selection with spinner interface

CalendarDatePicker.description: Represents a control that allows the user to pick a date from a calendar display or by entering text
CalendarDatePicker.xaml_example: <CalendarDatePicker Date="{Binding SelectedDate}" PlaceholderText="Select a date" IsCalendarOpen="{Binding IsCalendarOpen}"/>
CalendarDatePicker.usage: Flexible date selection with both text input and calendar popup

TimePicker.description: Represents a control that allows the user to select a time value
TimePicker.usage: Time selection with spinner or clock interface

AutoCompleteBox.description: Represents a control that provides a text box for user input and a drop-down that contains possible matches
AutoCompleteBox.xaml_example: <AutoCompleteBox ItemsSource="{Binding Suggestions}" Text="{Binding SearchText}" MinimumPrefixLength="2" FilterMode="StartsWith"/>

NumericUpDown.description: Represents a control for entering numeric values with up/down buttons
NumericUpDown.xaml_example: <NumericUpDown Value="{Binding Quantity}" Minimum="0" Maximum="100" Increment="1" FormatString="N0"/>

ToolTip.description: Represents a control that creates a pop-up window that displays information for an element
ToolTip.xaml_example: <Button Content="Hover me"><ToolTip.Tip><ToolTip Content="This is a tooltip with additional information"/></ToolTip.Tip></Button>
ToolTip.usage: Contextual help and additional information on hover

Flyout.description: Represents a control that displays lightweight UI that is either information, or requires user interaction

SplitView.description: Represents a container with two views; one view for the main content and another view that is typically used for navigation commands

ColorPicker.description: Represents a control that lets users pick a color using spectrum, palette, or component-based input
ColorPicker.usage: Color selection in design tools, theming panels, or any color configuration UI
